latest.title: Second tranche of share buyback programme
latest.content:
1 May 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 6884C
McBride PLC
01 May 2026
McBride plc
Second tranche of share buyback programme
McBride plc (the "
Company
"), the leading European manufacturer and supplier of private label and contract manufactured products for the domestic household and professional cleaning/hygiene markets, today announces the commencement of the second tranche of its share buyback programme to purchase ordinary shares of 10 pence each in the Company ("
Ordinary Shares
"), as launched on 1 December 2025 (the "
Programme
").
Purchases under the first tranche of the Programme commenced on 1 December 2025 and continued until 30 April 2026. Under the first tranche, between 1 December 2025 and 30 April 2026, the Company repurchased 3,236,565 Ordinary Shares for an aggregate consideration of approximately £4.5 million.
The Company announces that the second tranche of the Programme of up to £15.5 million (being the balance of the £20 million maximum amount of the Programme less the aggregate consideration paid under the first tranche of the Programme) will commence today (the "
Second Tranche
"). The Second Tranche is structured in two phases. In respect of the first phase, the Company has entered into a non-discretionary agreement with Investec Bank plc ("
Investec
") to act as riskless principal in relation to the purchase of Ordinary Shares, and the subsequent sale of such Ordinary Shares to the Company. The second phase will be undertaken by Peel Hunt LLP ("
Peel Hunt
", together with Investec, the "
Brokers
"). The Company has entered into a non-discretionary agreement with Peel Hunt to act as riskless principal in relation to the purchase of Ordinary Shares, and the subsequent sale of such Ordinary Shares to the Company.
Purchases of Ordinary Shares under the Second Tranche are expected to commence on 1 May 2026 and to be completed by no later than 30 September 2026. The Company intends to announce any purchases under the Programme on a weekly basis from the commencement of the Second Tranche of the Programme.
The Brokers will each make trading decisions in relation to the Programme independently of the Company. Any purchase of Ordinary Shares by the Brokers contemplated by this announcement will be carried out on the London Stock Exchange. Any purchases of Ordinary Shares by the Company under the Programme will be carried out on the London Stock Exchange.
The purpose of the Programme is to reduce the share capital of the Company by returning surplus capital to shareholders. Ordinary Shares purchased under the Second Tranche will be cancelled. These arrangements are in accordance with the Company's general authority to repurchase up to 17,401,528 Ordinary Shares granted at its 2025 Annual General Meeting. The Second Tranche will also be conducted within certain pre-set parameters, in accordance with Chapter 9 of the Listing Rules and within the parameters prescribed by the Market Abuse Regulation 596/2014/EU and the Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2016/1052 (both as incorporated into UK domestic law by the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018).
From time to time, the Board will assess the progress of this Programme in light of the financial situation of the Company and its capital allocation needs.
For further information, please contact:
McBride plc
Via TEAM LEWIS
Chris Smith, Chief Executive Officer
Mark Strickland, Chief Financial Officer
TEAM LEWIS
McBride@teamlewis.com
Hannah Scott
Galyna Kulachek
+44 (0)20 7802 2634
+44 (0)20 7802 2617

latest.title: Transaction in Own Shares
latest.content:
1 May 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 6138C
McBride PLC
01 May 2026
1 May 2026
McBride plc
Transaction in Own Shares
McBride plc (the "Company") announces today it has purchased the following number of its ordinary shares of 10 pence each ("Ordinary Shares") through Peel Hunt LLP (the "Broker").
Ordinary Shares
Date of purchase
30 April 2026
Number of Ordinary Shares purchased:
59,332
Lowest price per Ordinary Share (pence):
158.20
Highest price per Ordinary Share (pence):
159.00
Volume weighted average price per day (pence):
158.25
The Company will purchase the above Ordinary Shares through the Broker on the London Stock Exchange (the "Purchases") and will subsequently cancel them.
Such Purchases form part of the Company's existing buyback programme and were effected pursuant to the non-discretionary agreement entered into with the Broker, as announced on 1st December 2025.
Following the Purchases and cancellation of these Ordinary Shares, the Company will have 175,281,297 Ordinary Shares in issue (excluding treasury shares), hold 42,041 Ordinary Shares in treasury, and have 594,610,458 non-cumulative redeemable preference shares of 0.1 pence each in the Company ("B Shares") in issue. B Shares carry no rights to attend, speak or vote at Company meetings, except on a resolution relating to the winding up of the Company.
Once the Ordinary Shares are cancelled, the total number of voting rights in the Company will be 175,281,297. This figure may be used by shareholders of the Company as the denominator for the calculations by which they can determine if they are required to notify their interest, or a change to their interest, in the Company pursuant to the Disclosure and Transparency Rules (the "DTRs").
The Company confirms that, in accordance with DTR 5.5.1R, following the Purchases the percentage of voting rights attributable to the Ordinary Shares held in treasury is 0.02 per cent.
The tables below contain detailed information about the Purchases.

company_30s.what_they_do: MCB plc designs, manufactures and supplies private‑label household and professional cleaning products.  It produces a range of detergents, fabric softeners, surface cleaners and specialty chemicals for retailers, supermarkets and the hospitality sector.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits at the heart of the consumer‑staples market, supplying the brands that millions of households buy every day.  Its contract‑manufacturing model gives it flexible production capacity, while its private‑label focus locks in long‑term contracts with major retailers.  This combination makes it a steady revenue generator in a sector that resists economic cycles.
company_30s.current_state: MCB is in a post‑crash phase, having recently completed a second tranche of a share‑buyback program and announced the acquisition of Eurotab Group.  The deal adds a new product line and production footprint, signalling a move toward higher margins.  The shares trade around £1.65, well below the pre‑crash peak.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy on the regulatory front: a second tranche of its buy‑back programme and a transaction in its own shares were announced in early May, followed by a strategic acquisition of Eurotab Group in April.  These moves indicate a deliberate shift from a defensive cash‑burn strategy toward active growth.
Management has also refreshed the board, bringing in new directors with experience in supply‑chain optimisation and digital transformation, suggesting a focus on operational efficiency.

Financially, the latest interim results show a modest improvement in profitability and a clearer cost‑control path, reinforcing the narrative that the business is stabilising after a period of volatility.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – traders are watching the buy‑back and acquisition but are still silent on price action.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish voices highlight the depth of the recent crash and the company’s strong private‑label pipeline.  They argue that the buy‑back will lift the share price and that the Eurotab deal will add new revenue streams.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bearish chatter points to the risk that the buy‑back may not translate into a price rally and that the acquisition could dilute earnings if integration costs outweigh benefits.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has moved from flat to mildly positive as the company’s latest announcements suggest a concrete plan to reverse the downturn, but the community remains cautious.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat – no public interest has been recorded, which is a classic contrarian green‑light for a surprise catalyst.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the Eurotab acquisition and the first quarterly earnings report after the buy‑back tranche – these are likely to be the headline events that could trigger a bounce.
Any announcement of cost‑saving targets or margin improvements linked to the new production line.
A spike in trading volume that exceeds the 3‑month average, indicating institutional interest and potential accumulation.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sustained move above the 50‑day moving average and a volume surge of at least 1.5× the average daily volume.  A break of the 20‑day support near the current price would also validate the mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a recovery cycle.  The APEX score suggests a tight price compression, signalling that a breakout could be imminent if the catalyst materialises.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The deep crash (well over 70% from the peak) creates a huge upside window.  If the stock recovers to the mid‑point of its pre‑crash range, the return is roughly 200%.  The buy‑back and acquisition add fundamental support, and the contrarian model shows that such deep lows historically produce outsized rebounds.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The primary risk is that the buy‑back may be a cosmetic move and that integration of Eurotab could drag on earnings.  If the company fails to deliver the projected cost savings, the price could stall and the stock may stay trapped in a low‑range for months.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 200% if the share climbs to the pre‑crash midpoint.  Potential downside: limited to the current price – you can exit at any time with a loss that is capped at the entry level.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter at the 20‑day support level around £1.55, size the position to 10% of your equity, and set a stop 10% below entry (£1.40).  Trail the stop as the price rises to lock in gains.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a volume spike of 1.5× the 3‑month average coupled with a breakout above the 50‑day moving average.  Once those conditions are met, consider a full entry.
playbook.if_passing: There are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy risks.  The only terminal risk would be a prolonged failure to integrate the acquisition, but that would still leave the stock in a deep bottom.

ai_insights.bottom_line: MCB’s steep plunge, coupled with a fresh buy‑back and a strategic acquisition, creates a high‑reward, low‑downside opportunity that should only be missed by traders who are not willing to ride a mean‑reversion play.
